pattern: Tracking which letters appear in each case lets you intersect the two sets and report the alphabetically largest shared letter.
hints: You only care about letters that show up in both their lowercase and uppercase forms.
Collect every character into a set, then ask which alphabet letters have both cases present.
Walk from 'Z' down to 'A'; the first letter whose lowercase and uppercase both appear in the string is the answer, otherwise return an empty string.

edge_inputs.description: several shared letters, pick the greatest
no letter appears in both cases

stress.description: random mixed-case string forcing a scan over the alphabet
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, size)
    pool = string.ascii_letters
    return [''.join(rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n))]
stress.note: wide mix makes shared letters common
